name: hakana
description: 儚 — the fleeting colours of a thin film, born from nothing. A soap film's iridescence is not painted; it is computed from real optics. Light reflected from the film's two faces interferes wavelength by wavelength (Fresnel + Airy), and the surviving spectrum is turned into the eye's colour through the CIE 1931 matching functions under D65. The film is alive: it drains downward under gravity, swirls with marangoni flow, and opens a black film at the top just before it bursts — and the most beautiful colours appear the instant before it is gone. Deterministic from a seed; the core knows neither DOM nor network.
